name: How can I stay safe when hiring a handyman to work inside my home?
headline: How can I stay safe when hiring a handyman to work inside my home?

mainEntity.name: How can I stay safe when hiring a handyman to work inside my home?
mainEntity.text: How can I stay safe when hiring a handyman to work inside my home?

acceptedAnswer.text: It’s normal to worry about safety when you let someone work in your home. You can stay safe by **checking their background, setting clear boundaries, and trusting your instincts**. Safety steps include: - Read online reviews carefully, looking for patterns about trustworthiness, respect, and professionalism. - Ask friends, neighbors, or local groups if anyone has used them before. - Confirm their full name, business name, and contact details before they arrive. - Put away cash, jewelry, and small valuable items if it helps you feel more relaxed. When they arrive, notice: - Do they show up when they said they would, or contact you if they’re running late? - Are they respectful of your home, like using drop cloths or asking before moving furniture? Pitfalls: - Ignoring red flags, such as refusing to give any written estimate, pressuring you into extra work, or not respecting your personal space. - Letting someone continue working if you feel very uncomfortable. It is okay to stop the job and ask them to leave if you feel unsafe. You can also choose to have another adult at home during the first visit. Over time, as trust builds, many people feel comfortable giving their regular handyman more independence in their home.
